Number Square root Squared

164256
255625
1001010,000

What is a square root?

The square root of a number is the value that, when multiplied by itself, gives that number. For example, √25 = 5 because 5 × 5 = 25. Square roots and powers are inverse operations — squaring a number and taking its square root cancel each other out.

Frequently asked questions

What is the square root of 2?

The square root of 2 is approximately 1.41421356. It is an irrational number — its decimal digits go on forever without repeating. It is the length of the diagonal of a unit square.

Can you take the square root of a negative number?

Not in the real number system. The square root of a negative number is an imaginary number (using the imaginary unit i, where i² = −1). This is part of complex number mathematics.

What is the difference between x² and √x?

x² (x squared) multiplies x by itself. √x (square root of x) finds the number that when squared gives x. They are inverse operations: √(x²) = x and (√x)² = x for positive x.
